libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: For version 2.0.0, generated tests must import and reflect on jakarta.activation.* classes, not javax.activation.*. Keep tests focused on API-jar-reachable behavior unless intentionally testing the separate implementation artifact.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8769 [Automation] javac compile fails for jakarta.activation:jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: The jakarta.activation-api artifact is API-oriented and has no transitive implementation dependency; some registry-backed APIs such as MailcapCommandMap or MimetypesFileTypeMap reference com.sun.activation.registries classes that live in the separate implementation artifact. Avoid making those implementation-backed paths the only coverage for this API coordinate unless the workflow deliberately adds and accepts that separate implementation dependency.
com.sun.activation:jakarta.activation:2.0.0 is already a separate library coordinate in this repository, so adding it here could conflate API-artifact coverage with implementation-artifact coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: No extra Maven dependency, Docker image, environment variable, or system property is required before meaningful coverage is reachable for jakarta.activation:jakarta.activation-api:2.0.0. The javac failure is caused by generated tests using the old javax.activation namespace against the Jakarta 2.x artifact, whose classes are in jakarta.activation.
